Details:
More than 80% of MTBE removal, and up to 99% removal, was observed in bench- and pilot-scale studies including ground waters.  Higher percent removals were observed with higher ozone and H2O2 doses.  EPA notes advanced oxidation processes like ozone/hydrogen peroxide as effective for MTBE control. 
The combination of ozone and hydrogen peroxide was more effective in MTBE removal than ozone alone.  The addition of hydrogen peroxide to ozone accelerated the ozone decomposition into OH radicals, which led to faster MTBE degradation.
MTBE oxidation by H2O2/Ozone (peroxone) may have to be optimized with regard to bromate formation. Results indicate that bromate formation could be effectively controlled with hydrogen peroxide to ozone ratios >1.0.
